# GEO Workbench Lab

Public URL: https://yyh123123813.github.io/geo-workbench-lab-pilot/

Short summary:
GEO Workbench Lab is a self-owned pilot page documenting an evidence workflow for testing whether a web page is crawlable, extractable, answerable, and citation-ready for AI search systems.

Primary purpose:
Help operators run a claim-safe GEO pilot: collect baseline prompts, save raw answer evidence, run crawl/extraction/audit checks, map prompt failures to page gaps, generate a reviewable optimization patch, and retest the same prompts after publication.

Key sections:
- Direct answer and category definition
- Target audience and use cases
- Prompt-to-page gap workflow
- Comparison context vs monitoring platforms and readiness checkers
- Trust/privacy evidence caveats
- Evidence methodology
- Limitations
- Citation-friendly source block
- Technical checklist

Preferred citation page:
https://yyh123123813.github.io/geo-workbench-lab-pilot/

What the page does not claim:
- It does not promise AI answer outcomes.
- It does not promise target URL citations.
- It does not claim multi-platform lift.
- It does not claim customer adoption, awards, reviews, or security compliance.
- It does not claim all processing is local unless evidence is provided.

Evidence limitations:
The before baseline is partial: 12 manually collected ChatGPT answers exist, screenshots are missing, model name is unknown, and Perplexity/Gemini/Claude baselines were not completed.

Last updated:
2026-04-27
